ADC LLC — Formation Package
Step-by-step checklist · Operating Agreement · Patent Assignment · 2026-04-09

Step-by-Step — Do This Afternoon

  • 1
    Check name availability — Louisiana SOS
    Search "ADC" and your chosen full name to confirm it's available before filing. Takes 2 minutes.
    geauxbiz.sos.la.gov Free2 min
  • 2
    File Articles of Organization — Louisiana LLC
    Create account on GeauxBiz, select "Form a new business," choose LLC, enter your name and registered agent (you can be your own registered agent at your home address).
    geauxbiz.sos.la.gov ~$100 filing fee15–20 min
    Approved same day or next business day online. You'll get a Certificate of Organization by email.
  • 3
    Get your EIN (Federal Tax ID) — IRS
    Apply online immediately after state approval. Select "Limited Liability Company," sole member. Takes 10 minutes, EIN issued instantly on screen — screenshot it.
    irs.gov → Apply for EIN Online Free10 min
  • 4
    Open business bank account
    Bring: Certificate of Organization + EIN + your ID. Capital One Spark, Chase Business, or your local bank. Keep business money completely separate from personal from day one — critical for liability protection and investor credibility.
    Same day if you go in person
  • 5
    Sign the Operating Agreement (below)
    Fill in entity name, date, and EIN. Sign. Keep original. Louisiana does not require filing — this is an internal document. Print to PDF and save.
    No cost5 min
  • 6
    File Provisional Patent at USPTO
    Create account, start new application, select "Provisional Application." Upload the provisional patent HTML printed to PDF + the cassette floor plan SVG as the drawing. Pay filing fee.
    patentcenter.uspto.gov $80 micro / $160 small entity20–30 min
    You get a filing receipt with your Application Number immediately. That date is your priority date — 12-month clock starts now.
  • 7
    Sign the Patent Assignment (below)
    Fill in provisional application number from step 6. Sign. Record with USPTO within 3 months to protect against third-party claims.
    assignment.uspto.gov $0 electronic recording10 min
  • 8
    Register with Louisiana Department of Revenue
    For state sales tax and income tax. Required before you invoice any customer.
    latap.revenue.louisiana.gov Free15 min
  • 9
    Sign and send Fibrebond NDA
    Sign the NDA under the new ADC entity name. Send to Fibrebond before or at the visit. File is at adc3k-deploy/vendors/fibrebond/adc-fibrebond-nda.html — print to PDF.
Total cost today: ~$180–260  ·  Total time: 2–3 hours  ·  Result: Legal entity, federal tax ID, 12-month patent priority, bank account, NDA ready to sign

Operating Agreement — ADC LLC

Single-Member LLC Operating Agreement
Fill in highlighted blanks · Sign · Keep original · Do not file with state

This Operating Agreement (this "Agreement") of LLC (the "Company"), a Louisiana limited liability company, is entered into as of , 2026, by Scott Tomsu (the "Member").

Article 1 — Formation

The Company was formed as a Louisiana limited liability company upon the filing of its Articles of Organization with the Louisiana Secretary of State. The Company is governed by this Agreement and the Louisiana Limited Liability Company Law (La. R.S. 12:1301 et seq.).

Article 2 — Name and Principal Office

The name of the Company is LLC. The principal office of the Company is located at , Lafayette, Louisiana , or such other address as the Member may designate.

Article 3 — Purpose

The Company is organized for the purpose of designing, developing, licensing, and commercializing autonomous direct current AI compute infrastructure, including the ADC Cassette product line and the Pure DC AI Factory system architecture, and any other lawful business activity as determined by the Member.

Article 4 — Member and Ownership

Scott Tomsu is the sole Member of the Company and holds 100% of the membership interests. The Member's initial capital contribution is $. No other person has any membership interest in the Company unless admitted in accordance with this Agreement.

Article 5 — Management

The Company is member-managed. The Member has full authority to manage, control, and operate the business and affairs of the Company, to execute contracts and agreements on behalf of the Company, to open and manage bank accounts, and to take all other actions necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of the Company.

Article 6 — Profits, Losses, and Distributions

All profits and losses of the Company shall be allocated to the Member. Distributions shall be made to the Member at such times and in such amounts as the Member determines, subject to applicable law and the Company's obligations.

Article 7 — Intellectual Property

All intellectual property developed by the Member in connection with the Company's business, including without limitation the ADC Cassette product architecture, the Pure DC AI Factory system concept, any provisional or non-provisional patent applications, trademarks, trade secrets, and proprietary engineering specifications, shall be owned by the Company. The Member hereby assigns to the Company all right, title, and interest in and to any such intellectual property created or conceived in connection with the Company's business purposes.

Article 8 — Transfer of Membership Interests

The Member may transfer membership interests to any person or entity as the Member determines. The admission of additional members and the issuance of additional membership interests shall require an amendment to this Agreement signed by the Member.

Article 9 — Liability

The Member shall not be personally liable for any debts, obligations, or liabilities of the Company solely by reason of being a member, except as required by applicable law.

Article 10 — Dissolution

The Company shall dissolve upon the written determination of the Member or as required by applicable law. Upon dissolution, the Company's assets shall be applied first to creditors, then distributed to the Member.

Article 11 — Governing Law

This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Louisiana.

Article 12 — Entire Agreement

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the Member with respect to the Company and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings relating to the subject matter hereof.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Member has executed this Operating Agreement as of the date first written above.

Signature of Member
Printed Name: Scott Tomsu
Date
EIN: _______________________

Patent Assignment — Provisional Application

Assignment of Provisional Patent Application
Complete after USPTO filing · Record at assignment.uspto.gov

This Assignment of Provisional Patent Application (this "Assignment") is made as of , 2026, by Scott Tomsu, an individual residing in Lafayette, Louisiana (the "Assignor"), to LLC, a Louisiana limited liability company (the "Assignee").

WHEREAS, Assignor is the inventor and owner of a provisional patent application filed with the United States Patent and Trademark Office on , 2026, Application No. , titled Autonomous Direct Current Cassette System — Modular, Autonomously Controlled, Scalable AI Compute Infrastructure with Pure DC Architecture, Heterogeneous Configuration, and Four-Connection Site Interface (the "Application");

NOW THEREFORE, for good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, Assignor hereby assigns, transfers, and conveys to Assignee all right, title, and interest in and to:

  1. The Application and all inventions disclosed therein;
  2. Any non-provisional patent application claiming priority to the Application;
  3. Any patents issuing from the Application or any related application;
  4. Any continuations, continuations-in-part, divisionals, reissues, and reexaminations thereof; and
  5. All foreign patent applications and patents corresponding to any of the foregoing.

Assignor agrees to execute any further documents and take any further actions reasonably requested by Assignee to perfect, record, or enforce the rights assigned herein.

This Assignment is governed by the laws of the State of Louisiana.

Signature of Assignor (Scott Tomsu)
Date
Signature of Assignee (on behalf of ADC LLC)
Title: Member / Manager

Recording instructions: Go to assignment.uspto.gov → Record Assignment → enter Application Number → upload this signed document. Electronic recording is free and instant. You will receive a reel/frame number confirming recordation.

Note: This package was prepared by ADC for reference purposes and does not constitute legal advice. The operating agreement and patent assignment are standard single-member LLC documents suitable for initial formation. Scott recommends review by a Louisiana-licensed attorney before raising outside capital or adding members, at which point the operating agreement will need to be updated to reflect multi-member ownership and investor rights.  ·  scott@adc3k.com  ·  (337) 780-1535
